title: Simelane removed as justice minister but still in Cabinet
bodyText:
NATIONAL NEWS - President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced changes to the National Executive with immediate effect.

In a statement released last night, President Cyril Ramaphosa announced changes to four portfolios.

He said the changes have been made to ensure the effectiveness of Cabinet in delivering on its mandate and in accordance with section 91(2) of the Constitution.


> The president has appointed Mmamoloko Kubayi, currently the minister of human settlements, as the minister of justice and constitutional development.


Thembi Simelane, currently the minister of justice and constitutional development, has been appointed as the minister of human settlements.

Simelane has faced criticism over her alleged involvement in the VBS Mutual Bank looting scandal. Media reports claim that Simelane, during her tenure as Polokwane mayor in Limpopo, received a R575 000 loan from a company closely tied to VBS Bank.

Phumzile Mgcina has been appointed the deputy minister of mineral and petroleum resources. Mgcina has been serving in the National Executive as the deputy minister of labour and employment.

Judith Nemadzinga-Tshabalala has been appointed as deputy minister of employment and labour.

Nemadzinga-Tshabalala has been serving in the National Executive as deputy minister of Mineral and Petroleum Resources.


> “The changes to the National Executive are effective immediately. I would like to wish these members of the national executive well in their new positions,” said the president.


The Constitution vests executive authority of the Republic in the president.

The president exercises executive authority with other members of Cabinet in fulfilling various provisions of the Constitution and national legislation. – SAnews.gov.za

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
